BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Break-even analysis for Perez Textile Conservation, sole proprietorship. Models a potential studio-rent increase during 2027 lease renewal against current retainer-client capacity.

CurrentState.Note: Instructor pay line covers Samuel Okafor's 15 hours per week at $22/hour. Membership dues line represents active retainer clients (MFA, Peabody Essex, Ashworth-referred private collector). Drop-ins represent project-fee visits.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: At current rent ($2,400/mo) and retainer mix (MFA, Peabody Essex, private collectors via Amina Rashid), break-even sits at ~3 active retainer clients. A rent bump to $2,750 would require one additional steady retainer or a $350/month project-fee increase to hold margin.
BreakEvenAnalysis.PreparedBy: David Leung, Leung and Associates CPA

Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: 5635 total expenses / 1800 per retainer client = 3.13 rounded up to 4
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Requires one additional steady retainer or +$350/mo in project fees to hold current margin.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent to $2,750/mo (modest 2027 renewal)

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Same 4-retainer break-even; margin compresses to $1,065/mo. Consider renegotiating with landlord.
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Rent to $2,850/mo (aggressive 2027 renewal)
